incidents.summary: On November 19, 2025, Mount Semeru in Indonesia erupted, sending ash more than 6,500 feet into the sky and threatening air traffic. The eruption prompted the rescue of over 170 stranded climbers, with authorities evacuating more than 900 people in total.

logs.summary: Typhoon Bavi (local name Inday) weakened into a typhoon early Thursday, with Tropical Cyclone Wind Signal No. 1 still in effect over several areas in Northern Luzon, Philippines.

news.headline: 'Inday' weakens into typhoon; Signal No. 1 still up

logs.summary: Super Typhoon Bavi (Inday) intensified to a Category 5 storm, pounding the U.S. Northern Mariana Islands and Guam with catastrophic winds. The storm is now expected to weaken as it threatens northern Taiwan and Japan's Ryukyu Islands. Separately, landslides in the Philippines attributed to Bavi have killed 15 people.

logs.summary: Typhoon Bavi weakened from a super typhoon to a typhoon as it moved over the Philippine Sea, with Signal No. 2 raised in parts of northern Luzon and enhanced monsoon rains causing at least 10 deaths and affecting nearly 50,000 people in the Philippines. The storm then churned toward Taiwan and China's east coast, prompting evacuations and preparations for what forecasters warned could be the most powerful typhoon to hit the region in years.

logs.summary: As Typhoon Bavi approached China’s eastern coast, authorities evacuated over 900,000 people and issued an orange alert, suspending flights and trains in affected areas. In the Philippines, the death toll rose to at least 17 with nine missing due to the typhoon and enhanced monsoon rains, while the storm moved toward southern Japan and continued to batter Taiwan and Japan’s southern islands.

logs.summary: Typhoon Bavi made landfall in eastern China’s Zhejiang province on July 12, 2026, weakening to a severe tropical storm as it moved inland. Authorities evacuated 1.7 to nearly 3 million people ahead of the storm, which brought heavy rainfall and widespread flight and railway cancellations across the Yangtze River Delta region. The storm triggered red rainstorm alerts in parts of China and continued to cause flooding and infrastructure damage.

incidents.summary: Wildfires continue to rage in Portugal, Spain, and Greece following a June heatwave that caused thousands of excess deaths. Hundreds of firefighters are battling the blazes, and Portugal is exploring a shift from fire suppression to prevention by improving forest management, described as a "fire paradox."

logs.summary: Major wildfires are burning across southern France, prompting the evacuation of around 3,000 people as an early fire season hits Europe. The blazes have also threatened the Tour de France, with stage 3 possibly being cancelled due to extreme temperatures and fire activity.

logs.summary: The wildfire in southern France has intensified, forcing the evacuation of over 10,000 people and scorching more than 11,000 acres (over 1,600 hectares). A 22-year-old volunteer firefighter died while battling the blaze. The third stage of the Tour de France proceeded with an adapted format, closed to the public due to the fire risk.

logs.summary: Heavy rainfall since mid-June triggered flooding in southern China's Guangxi region and Guangdong province, forcing relocations and disrupting transport. logs.summary:
As of July 14, China's National Meteorological Center issued an orange alert for rainstorms, noting that 169 tailings ponds are affected by rainfall. Typhoon Bavi, the strongest storm to hit mainland China this year, forced over 260,000 people to evacuate their homes in Liaoning province. Additionally, videos surfaced of hundreds of snakes escaping into floodwaters, reportedly after Typhoon Maysak.

Record rainfall in China led to residents paddleboarding and swimming through flooded streets. Floodwaters swept away vehicles in Chengde following Typhoon Bavi, while emergency workers reinforced dikes in Shenyang. Torrential rains and widespread flooding continued to disrupt life across eastern China.

incidents.summary: The first cases of H5N1 bird flu have been detected in Australian seabirds. A confirmed case was found in a brown skua in Cape Le Grand, Western Australia, and a suspected case in a giant petrel near Hawks Nest, New South Wales. Additionally, CSIRO lab testing confirmed the strain in a greater crested tern.

incidents.canonical_name: Hantavirus cluster linked to cruise ship travel, Multi-country
incidents.summary: A Canadian passenger tested positive for Andes hantavirus after returning from the cruise ship, making Canada the fourth country with a confirmed case linked to the outbreak. The U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention ended its response to the hantavirus cluster, and all 18 U.S. passengers from the MV Hondius returned to their home states. Additionally, an American passenger, Angela Perryman, was ordered by Health Secretary RFK Jr. to remain in quarantine against her will, sparking a controversy over her confinement.

The fire has scorched approximately 66 square kilometers (25 square miles) of forest and farmland, with hundreds of firefighters continuing to battle the blaze. Additionally, discussions have begun about implementing bushfire bunkers as a safety measure in Spain.

logs.summary: A fast-moving wildfire in southern Spain's Almería province, starting around July 9-10, 2026, killed at least 13 people and left over 20 missing, many of them foreign nationals (including British and Belgian). The fire, which prompted evacuations of roughly 800 people, is suspected to have been ignited by a broken power line amid a severe summer heatwave. It is considered one of Spain's deadliest wildfires on record.

logs.summary: Spanish authorities identified all 13 victims of the wildfire, confirming 12 were foreign nationals: seven from the UK, three from Belgium, and one each from France and the US. This identification occurred five days after the fire ravaged a remote expat community in Almería.

logs.summary: New details emerged about the Almería wildfire, with reports indicating that victims were killed while attempting to escape along a river bed. The fire has scorched approximately 66 square kilometers (25 square miles) of forest and farmland, with hundreds of firefighters continuing to battle the blaze. Additionally, discussions have begun about implementing bushfire bunkers as a safety measure in Spain.

incidents.summary: Severe drought and heat across Europe are shrinking harvests, notably French maize, and causing water shortages in the Netherlands. The Rhine River has hit its lowest July level for decades, threatening energy and logistics, while Greek islands face drought during the tourist season; economists warn this extreme heat could raise food prices next year.

incidents.canonical_name: Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus, Democratic Republic of the Congo & Uganda
incidents.summary: Seven American aid workers who had been combating the ongoing Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo are now quarantining at a facility in Kenya following a US travel ban. This development highlights ongoing international response and containment efforts related to the Bundibugyo virus outbreak.
